title: Nassau 2-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Nassau 2-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Nassau, Bahamas: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Atlantis Paradise Island Aquaventure Water Park and The Dig at Atlantis. Prices from $615 per person (estimate).
highlights: Atlantis Paradise Island Aquaventure Water Park
Private overwater bungalow stay at Atlantis
Sunset catamaran cruise along the coast
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Nassau, Bahamas — Tropical climate, warm year-round (highs 27-32°C) with a wetter, more humid summer and a real hurricane risk from June to November, peaking August-October. December to April is the dry season with warm days, lower humidity and no hurricane risk, making it the most popular and priciest window. This itinerary blends Atlantis Paradise Island Aquaventure Water Park, The Dig at Atlantis, Cable Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Atlantis Paradise Island Aquaventure Water Park, The Dig at Atlantis, Cable Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Nassau
itinerary.description: Land at Lynden Pindling International Airport (NAS) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Atlantis Paradise Island Aquaventure Water Park — Massive water park with slides, a lazy river and marine habitats. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Atlantis Paradise Island Aquaventure Water Park

itinerary.title: Nassau Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to The Dig at Atlantis — Aquarium-style marine exhibit walk-through beneath the resort. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to NAS for your onward flight.

travelTips: US dollars are accepted 1:1 everywhere, so there's rarely a need to exchange for Bahamian dollars.
Book Aquaventure Water Park tickets online — day-pass access for non-Atlantis guests is limited and sells out.
Visit Arawak Cay ("Fish Fry") for far more authentic, cheaper Bahamian food than most resort restaurants.
Time a trip outside the June-November hurricane season if flexible, especially avoiding August-October.
A swimming-pigs day trip to Exuma is a full-day commitment by small plane or boat — book well ahead.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight, breathable clothing plus a cover-up for leaving the beach
Reef-safe sunscreen — reef protection rules apply at some sites
A light rain jacket for sudden tropical showers
US dollars are accepted everywhere so minimal currency exchange is needed
thingsToDo: Atlantis Paradise Island Aquaventure Water Park
Ardastra Gardens and Wildlife Conservation Centre
Swimming pigs day trip (Exuma, from Nassau)
thingsToAvoid: Wandering away from tourist areas of downtown Nassau alone after dark — petty crime rises off the main strip.
Skipping travel insurance during hurricane season — cancellations and delays are common June-November.
Overpaying at cruise-port souvenir stalls without a bit of friendly haggling at the Straw Market.
Touching or standing on coral reefs while snorkeling — it damages fragile ecosystems and can cut skin.
Assuming resort-fee inclusions — read the fine print, as many Nassau resorts add mandatory daily resort fees.
topRestaurants: Arawak Cay conch shacks — Bahamian Seafood ($)
Fish Fry conch stands — Conch Salad/Fritters ($)
adventureActivities: Swimming with pigs day trip to Exuma
Snorkeling and diving Bahamian reefs and wrecks
luxuryExperiences: Private overwater bungalow stay at Atlantis
Private yacht charter to the Exuma Cays
High-stakes evening at the Atlantis Casino
Fine dining tasting menu at Graycliff
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset catamaran cruise along the coast
Private beach dinner on Blue Lagoon Island
Couples spa day at an Atlantis resort
Swim with dolphins experience for two
budgetExperiences: Free swim at Junkanoo or Cable Beach
Conch fritters and salad at Arawak Cay for a few dollars
Climb the free Queen's Staircase and Fort Fincastle
Wander Bay Street and the Straw Market

festivalCalendar.name: Junkanoo
festivalCalendar.timing: Boxing Day (Dec 26) and New Year's Day parades

festivalCalendar.name: Great Exuma Regatta (nearby island, popular add-on)

localEtiquette: Greet with "Good morning/afternoon" before starting any transaction — it's expected local courtesy.
Tipping 15% is standard at restaurants; many resorts add an automatic service/resort fee — check your bill.
Modest cover-ups are expected off the beach — swimwear is for the beach/pool only, not town streets.
"Bahamian time" runs relaxed — service and buses may run behind schedule; patience is expected.
Straw market vendors expect polite haggling on crafts, but fixed-price shops do not.
localTransportGuide: Jitney buses (privately run local buses) are the cheapest way around New Providence island but run on loose schedules; taxis are metered or fixed-rate and widely available at hotels and the cruise port. Many visitors base themselves at a resort and book excursions directly rather than relying on public transport.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April is the dry season with warm days, lower humidity and no hurricane risk, making it the most popular and priciest window.
weather: Tropical climate, warm year-round (highs 27-32°C) with a wetter, more humid summer and a real hurricane risk from June to November, peaking August-October.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for tourist stays up to 90 days; the US dollar is accepted everywhere alongside the pegged Bahamian dollar. Indian passport holders should check current requirements — a valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa often facilitates visa-free or visa-on-arrival entry.
averageFlightTime: ~15.5h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Nassau?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) for tourist stays up to 90 days; the US dollar is accepted everywhere alongside the pegged Bahamian dollar. Indian passport holders should check current requirements — a valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa often facilitates visa-free or visa-on-arrival entry.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Nassau?
faqs.answer: December to April is the dry season with warm days, lower humidity and no hurricane risk, making it the most popular and priciest window. Tropical climate, warm year-round (highs 27-32°C) with a wetter, more humid summer and a real hurricane risk from June to November, peaking August-October.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Nassau safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Nassau is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Nassau?
faqs.answer: The local currency is BSD. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Nassau is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset catamaran cruise along the coast experience. It also works well for families — highlights include Aquaventure Water Park at Atlantis.

reviews.author: James Kim
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Nassau was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Ardastra Gardens and Wildlife Conservation Centre was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Elena Kim
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Arawak Cay ("Fish Fry") and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Wei Williams
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Nassau catamaran/snorkel day cruise in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Wei Novak
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Graycliff Chocolatier tour and tasting in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mia Novak
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Pirates of Nassau Museum and Junkanoo Beach. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.
